cells.label: cl1/2 concord
cells.function: human class governs associative wa / subject a-,wa-

conditioning: noun-class (the controller's class selects the concord marker)

examples: nouns whose assigned class is uncertain take a non-dominant marker
fit_none.note: a minority of tokens take a non-dominant concord marker — concord exceptions or mis-assigned nouns

evidence_citations.claim: each class governs a dominant concord marker
evidence_citations.stat: associative votes: u->wa .85, m->wa .81, ki->cha .83, vi->vya .86, ji->la .80

evidence_citations.claim: 13/14 classes have a clean dominant marker
evidence_citations.stat: 13 of 14 voting classes have a >=0.5-share dominant associative marker

prose: swh shows productive NOUN-CLASS CONCORD: every class governs a dominant associative agreement marker on its modifiers (cl7 ki->cha .83, cl8 vi->vya .86, cl5 ji->la .80, cl1/2 m->wa .81, cl11/14 u->wa .85). 13 of 14 classes have a clean dominant marker; a minority of non-dominant votes are concord exceptions or mis-assigned nouns. This concord is the agreement-layer confirmation of the noun-class inventory. Recommendation: PROMOTE the class->concord mapping.
